user_message: Need help with VanArsdel Ltd -- map the stakeholders. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 14:22 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 16:07 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 13:28 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-02-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:12:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:17:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:29:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:33:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:36:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-E trap (Misses the most recent thread); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-E failure mode (Misses the most recent thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
